title: The Winsome Wee Thing.
content:
I.

        She is a winsome wee thing,
        She is a handsome wee thing,
        She is a bonnie wee thing,
            This sweet wee wife o' mine.

II.

        I never saw a fairer,
        I never lo'ed a dearer;
        And niest my heart I'll wear her,
            For fear my jewel tine.

III.

        She is a winsome wee thing,
        She is a handsome wee thing,
        She is a bonnie wee thing,
            This sweet wee wife o' mine.

IV.

        The warld's wrack we share o't,
        The warstle and the care o't;
        Wi' her I'll blythely bear it,
            And think my lot divine.
meaning:
"The Winsome Wee Thing." is a quintessential example of Robert Burns's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
